feed.title: AATF AI News Aggregator - Research (Top 100)

author.name: AATF AI News Aggregator

entry.title: Research Summary: July 24, 2026

author.name: AATF AI News Aggregator

entry.summary:
Analysis complete. Top items selected by score.

Related Coverage

  • SLAI T-Rex: Full-Parameter Post-training of the DeepSeek-V4 Family on Ascend SuperPOD
  • Self Gradient Forcing: Native Long Video Extrapolation
  • Beyond Relevance-Centric Retrieval: Rubric-Oriented Document Set Selection and Ranking
  • An Exam for Active Observers
entry.content:
Analysis complete. Top items selected by score.

Related Coverage

  • SLAI T-Rex: Full-Parameter Post-training of the DeepSeek-V4 Family on Ascend SuperPOD
  • Self Gradient Forcing: Native Long Video Extrapolation
  • Beyond Relevance-Centric Retrieval: Rubric-Oriented Document Set Selection and Ranking
  • An Exam for Active Observers

entry.title: SLAI T-Rex: Full-Parameter Post-training of the DeepSeek-V4 Family on Ascend SuperPOD

author.name: Dongfang Li, Xiaodong Luo, Ruoyu Sun, Xuhui Chen, Linyuan Qiu, Jian Meng, Zhengxuan Lu, Yiting Wang, Yucheng Xie, Tao Guo, Tianxiang Fang, Jing Li, Sihang Chen, Shihao Hong, Chang Liu, Weihua Dai, Zirong Zeng, Ziwei Zhu, Zhuohan Wang, Zhengjun Yue, Igor Vasilyev, Min Liu, Weijian Sun, Xin Chen, Yingmeng Gao, Jinhua Zhou, Taolue Chen, Chenwei Wu, Dong Zhang, Wenlong Jin, Jinmin Xiang, Barkova Maria, Ushakov Anton, Xianfei Jin, Tian Ding, Zhihang Lin, Qian Chen, Linxin Yang, Mingzhe Yang, Bingwei Zhang, Hongzhang Yang, Fangxue Zhang, Shijun Qin, Jie Yu, Cuihua Hu, Tolstykh Vasiliy, Nosov Ivan, Abdullin Amir, Zhichen Zhou, Xin Zhang, Zhixiong Ning, Xutong Zhao, Junjie Huang, Jiajun Liu, Weiyan Kong, Zheng Zhang, Wenhan Luo, Lin Hu, Yangbo Guo, Li Zeng, Shihao Zeng, Baotian Hu, Min Zhang, Haizhou Li, Zhiquan Luo

entry.summary: Full-parameter post-training of trillion-parameter-scale MoE models introduces substantial system-level challenges for large-scale distributed training, including severe memory pressure, non-overlappe...

entry.title: Self Gradient Forcing: Native Long Video Extrapolation

author.name: Junhao Zhuang, Shiyi Zhang, Yuxuan Bian, Yaowei Li, Yawen Luo, Yijun Liu, Weiyang Jin, Songchun Zhang, Xianglong He, Xuying Zhang, Haoran Li, Haoyang Huang, Zeyue Xue, Nan Duan

entry.summary: Recent autoregressive video diffusion methods are increasingly built upon Self Forcing, where the student is trained on histories produced by its own rollout rather than ground-truth video contexts. T...

entry.title: Beyond Relevance-Centric Retrieval: Rubric-Oriented Document Set Selection and Ranking

author.name: Kailin Jiang, Lei Liu, Jian Xi, Hui Xu, Junlin Liu, Baochen Fu, Shaoqing Ren, Bin Li, Vichwang, Yu Lu, Haibo Shi

entry.summary: As large language models and AI agents become the primary consumers of search results, document set quality determines the upper bound of downstream generation. Yet existing evaluation systems remain ...

entry.title: An Exam for Active Observers

author.name: Jiarui Zhang, Muzi Tao, Shangshang Wang, Ollie Liu, Xuezhe Ma, Willie Neiswanger

entry.summary: Human vision is a closed loop: gaze is continuously redirected by intermediate hypotheses rather than a single snapshot. Decades of psychophysics and cognitive science have argued that this active obs...

entry.title: Scaling Laws for Hypernetwork-Based Knowledge Injection in Large Language Models

author.name: Nischay Dhankhar, Dos Baha, Abulhair Saparov

entry.summary: Injecting factual knowledge into large language models (LLMs) reliably and at scale remains an open challenge. Hypernetworks provide a promising solution to large-scale knowledge injection. Although h...

entry.title: Generalizable VLA Finetuning via Representation Anchoring and Language-Action Alignment

author.name: Dwip Dalal, Shivansh Patel, Chahit Jain, Jeonghwan Kim, Utkarsh Mishra, Alex Baratian, Hyeonjeong Ha, Heng Ji, Svetlana Lazebnik, Unnat Jain

entry.summary: Finetuning a pretrained vision-language model (VLM) on robot demonstrations via behavior cloning (BC) has become the standard recipe for vision-language-action (VLA) policies. However, BC finetuning p...

entry.title: Beyond Euclidean Clipping: Overcoming Exploration Collapse in LLM RL via Riemannian Isometric Policy Optimization

author.name: Zhicheng Cai, Xinyuan Guo, Hanlin Wu, Mingxuan Wang, Wei-Ying Ma, Ya-Qin Zhang, Hao Zhou

entry.summary: Reinforcement learning (RL) has become a dominant paradigm for enhancing LLMs' reasoning capabilities. However, RL algorithms with PPO-Clip are inherently limited by exploration collapse. Subsequent w...

entry.title: DocOps: A Verifiable Benchmark for Autonomous Agents in Complex Document Operations

author.name: Jiazhen Jiang, Boxi Cao, Lingyong Yan, Yaojie Lu, Hongyu Lin, Shuaiqiang Wang, Dawei Yin, Xianpei Han, Le Sun

entry.summary: As autonomous agents rapidly evolve, their ability to reliably manipulate ubiquitous digital documents has become critical for enabling general-purpose AI assistants and automating complex workspace w...

entry.title: ICAE-Bench: Evaluating Coding Agents as Interactive Project Builders

author.name: Zhongyuan Peng, Dan Huang, Chuyu Zhang, Caijun Xu, Changyi Xiao, Shibo Hong, David Lo, Lin Qiu, Xuezhi Cao, Jiyuan He, Yixin Cao

entry.summary: The recent emergence of vibe-coding workflows is changing what coding agents are expected to do. Instead of merely completing code under fully specified instructions, agents are increasingly expected ...

entry.title: ATSplat: Compact Feed-forward 3D Gaussian Splatting with Adaptive Token Expansion

author.name: Cho In, Jeonghwan Cho, Mijin Yoo, Gim Hee Lee, Seon Joo Kim

entry.summary: 3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS) achieves high-quality novel-view synthesis by optimizing freely placed primitives in 3D and adaptively densifying them in under-reconstructed regions. However, this scene-...

entry.title: FVAttn: Adaptive Sparse Attention with Runtime Load Balancing for Video Generation

author.name: Hao Liu, Chenghuan Huang, Ye Huang, Zhiying Wen, Hao Liu, Mohan Zhang, Chen Li, Ziyang Ma, Jing Lyu, Jiangsu Du

entry.summary: Video Diffusion Transformers process long spatio-temporal sequences, making self-attention the main bottleneck in high-resolution video generation. Training-free sparse attention reduces this cost, bu...

entry.title: Train the Model, Not the Reader: Decodability Supervision for Verifiable Activation Explanations

author.name: Hiskias Dingeto

entry.summary: Natural-language autoencoders score explanations of hidden activations by reconstruction: an explanation is deemed faithful if the activation can be regenerated from it. The test is structurally insen...

entry.title: Trace: A Taxonomy-Guided Environment for Multidomain Visual Reasoning

author.name: Md Tanvirul Alam

entry.summary: Reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards (RLVR) has substantially improved language-model reasoning, yet its extension to vision-language models remains constrained by the lack of training data ...

entry.title: SLPO: Scaling Latent Reasoning via a Surrogate Policy

author.name: Runyang You, Zhiyuan Liu, Yongqi Li, Wenjie Li

entry.summary: Reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards has become the predominant recipe for eliciting test-time scaling in explicit Chain-of-Thought reasoners. Yet this scaling path remains computationally c...

entry.title: G-MAD: A Game-Based Data Generation Framework for Multi-View RGB-T Aerial Object Detection

author.name: Yechan Kim, JongHyun Park, Dongho Yoon, Namhoon Jung, Moongu Jeon

entry.summary: This work introduces G-MAD, an open-source framework that uses Arma3 to generate synchronized multi-view RGB-T data for aerial object detection. G-MAD addresses key limitations of real-world aerial da...

entry.title: ENTRAP-VL: A Taxonomic Probe for Dual Contextual Entrainment in Vision-Language Models

author.name: Karan Goyal, Afreen Hossain, Debojyoti Das, Vishal Bhutani

entry.summary: Contextual entrainment is the tendency of a model to let auxiliary context in its input pull its output, independently of whether that context is relevant, true, or even meaningful. Recently, it has b...

entry.title: SeededGrasp: Language-Guided Grasping in Complex Scenes with Multiple Embodiments

author.name: Yang Xu, Gurpreet Singh Mukker, Raymond Wang, Jasper Gerigk, Maria Attarian, Igor Gilitschenski

entry.summary: Practical robotic grasping in complex scenes requires both 3D spatial reasoning and alignment with task-specific requirements. Vision-language models (VLMs) offer a natural way to specify these requir...

entry.title: Differentiable Logic Gate Networks for Low-Latency EEG Classification on Edge Devices

author.name: Shyamal Y. Dharia, Stephen D. Smith, Camilo E. Valderrama

entry.summary: Real-time EEG classification on edge devices is bottlenecked by the floating-point arithmetic of conventional neural networks. We investigated Differentiable Logic Gate Networks (Diff-Logic) as a hard...

entry.title: Research Summary: July 23, 2026

author.name: AATF AI News Aggregator

entry.summary:
Analysis complete. Top items selected by score.

Related Coverage

  • Masked Visual Actions for Unified World Modeling
  • ABot-World-0: Infinite Interactive World Rollout on a Single Desktop GPU
  • Where Should Optimizer State Live? Tiered State Allocation for Memory-Efficient Mixture-of-Experts Training
  • Asymptotically Optimal Regret for Reinforcement Learning without Horizon Dependence
entry.content:
Analysis complete. Top items selected by score.

Related Coverage

  • Masked Visual Actions for Unified World Modeling
  • ABot-World-0: Infinite Interactive World Rollout on a Single Desktop GPU
  • Where Should Optimizer State Live? Tiered State Allocation for Memory-Efficient Mixture-of-Experts Training
  • Asymptotically Optimal Regret for Reinforcement Learning without Horizon Dependence

entry.title: Masked Visual Actions for Unified World Modeling

author.name: Hadi Alzayer, Wenlong Huang, Haonan Chen, Christopher Luey, Lvmin Zhang, Maneesh Agrawala, Gordon Wetzstein, Li Fei-Fei, Yilun Du, Jiajun Wu, Jia-Bin Huang

entry.summary: Masked Visual Actions (MVA) introduces a pixel-space control interface for video world models, expressing action as a partially revealed trajectory of an arbitrary entity. Revealing robot motion makes the model predict scene response (forward dynamics); revealing desired object motion makes it recover robot behavior (inverse dynamics). Fine-tuned with only 15 hours of manipulation data, it unifies forward/inverse modeling.

entry.title: ABot-World-0: Infinite Interactive World Rollout on a Single Desktop GPU

author.name: Fan Jiang, Zhaoxu Sun, Mengchao Wang, Ziyu Zhu, Chiyu Wang, Yunpeng Zhang, Wenlin Liu, Yun Wang, Xue Zheng, Rui Sun, Junfeng Ni, Hongyu Pan, Zhongxu Sun, Fei Yu, Zengye Ge, Mengmeng Du, Nianfei Fan, Mingchao Sun, Yu Liu, Yongchang, Yanqing Zhu, Jiahang Wang, Ning Ying, Yuze Xuan, Di Yang, Zhicheng Liu, Zhe Gao, Tingbing Xu, Jiacheng Sui, Wenjin Yang, Junnan Lai, Shufeng Liu, Yuan Liu, Zheng Zhou, Yingliang Peng, Dawei Cao, Kaifeng Sheng, Yuxiang Cai, Fei Lu, Mu Xu, Ning Guo

entry.summary: ABot-World-0 presents an action-conditioned video world model enabling real-time, long-horizon closed-loop interaction on a single desktop GPU. It uses multi-source data from AAA games, simulations, and internet videos, with a unified pipeline applying 14 quality checks and VLM-based assessment. The model progressively distills a bidirectional teacher into a causal student using teacher forcing and ODE distillation, with LongForcing to align long self-rollouts and mitigate distribution shift.

entry.title: Where Should Optimizer State Live? Tiered State Allocation for Memory-Efficient Mixture-of-Experts Training

author.name: Nuemaan Malik

entry.summary: SkewAdam reduces optimizer state memory for MoE training by 97.4% (50.6 GB → 1.29 GB) and peak training memory from 81.4 GB to 31.3 GB. It assigns different state configurations to MoE's three parameter populations: float32 momentum + factored second moment for dense backbone (5% params), factored second moment alone for experts (95%), exact second moment for router (<0.01%).

entry.title: Asymptotically Optimal Regret for Reinforcement Learning without Horizon Dependence

author.name: Runlong Zhou, Zihan Zhang, Maryam Fazel, Simon S. Du

entry.summary: This paper proves asymptotically optimal horizon-free regret for finite-horizon tabular MDPs: Õ(√(SAK) + S⁸A³), completely removing log H dependence from prior Õ(√(SAK log H) + S²A log H) and drastically improving prior horizon-free Õ(√(S⁹A³K)). Matches contextual bandit lower bound Ω(√(SAK)) up to log factors.

entry.title: ISO: An RLVR-Native Optimization Stack

author.name: Hanqing Zhu, Wenyan Cong, Zhizhou Sha, Sagnik Mukherjee, Xinyuan Song, David González-Martínez, Xiaoxia Wu, Yuandong Tian, Shiwei Liu, David Z. Pan, Zhangyang "Atlas" Wang

entry.summary: ISO (Isospectral Optimization) is an RLVR-native optimization framework building on the discovery of spectral inheritance: RLVR reuses base model weight spectra while acquiring new behaviors through changes in input/output singular frames. ISO-Merger combines frame changes of specialists offline; ISO-Online applies fixed-spectrum updates online. This rethinks the optimization layer converting reward feedback to weight updates.

entry.title: The World Model Remembers, the Actor Forgets: Dream Rehearsal for Continual Model-Based RL

author.name: Gurp Nijjer

entry.summary: In DreamerV3 continual RL, the world model retains everything measurable about old tasks (reward discrimination ~1.0, value estimates, termination structure) while the actor's behavior collapses. Forgetting is a channel problem, not memory. Freezing world model with identical imagined rollouts and retraining actor recovers performance.

entry.title: Mage-Flow: An Efficient Native-Resolution Foundation Model for Image Generation and Editing

author.name: Xinjie Zhang, Peng Zhang, Shicheng Zheng, Jinghao Guo, Zhaoyang Jia, Yifei Shen, Xun Guo, Yuxuan Luo, Jiahao Li, Wenxuan Xie, Fanyi Pu, Xiaoyi Zhang, Kaichen Zhang, Zongyu Guo, Tianci Bi, Dongnan Gui, Zhening Liu, Zimo Wen, Zihan Zheng, Senqiao Yang, Xiao Li, Jinglu Wang, Bin Li, Yan Lu

entry.summary: Mage-Flow introduces a compact 4B-scale generative stack for efficient text-to-image generation and instruction-based editing. It co-designs Mage-VAE (lightweight latent tokenizer with one-step diffusion encoding/decoding and anchor-latent regularization) with a Native-Resolution Multimodal Diffusion Transformer trained via rectified flow matching. Achieves >10x tokenization cost reduction with native-resolution packing and CUDA kernel fusion.

entry.title: Robots Acquire Manipulation Skills in Seconds from a Single Human Video

author.name: Guangyan Chen, Meiling Wang, Te Cui, Zichen Zhou, Qi Shao, Shalfun Li, Hang Su, Roy Gan, Hao Wang, Mengyin Fu, Yi Yang, Yufeng Yue

entry.summary: HOST (Human-to-robot One-Shot Skill Acquisition) enables robots to acquire novel manipulation skills from a single human video in ~29 seconds, achieving 62% average success on 50 novel tasks while preserving previously mastered skills without policy parameter updates.

entry.title: Text Template Tokens Are Implicit Semantic Registers in Diffusion Transformers

author.name: Maohua Li, Qirui Li, Yanke Zhou, Yiduo Li, Zhaosheng Chi, Chao Xu, Cuifeng Shen, Yixuan Xu, Hanlin Tang, Kan Liu, Tao Lan, Lin Qu, Shao-Qun Zhang

entry.summary: This paper introduces a causal interpretability framework for diffusion transformers (DiTs) combining attention decomposition with targeted interventions. It discovers that structural template tokens, despite carrying little prompt-specific information at encoder output, emerge as dominant image-to-text attention sinks and act as implicit semantic registers maintaining object identity. These tokens acquire identity indirectly through prompt semantics injected into image latents.

entry.title: SLAI T-Rex: Full-Parameter Post-training of the DeepSeek-V4 Family on Ascend SuperPOD

author.name: Dongfang Li, Xiaodong Luo, Ruoyu Sun, Xuhui Chen, Linyuan Qiu, Jian Meng, Zhengxuan Lu, Yiting Wang, Yucheng Xie, Tao Guo, Tianxiang Fang, Jing Li, Sihang Chen, Shihao Hong, Chang Liu, Weihua Dai, Zirong Zeng, Ziwei Zhu, Zhuohan Wang, Zhengjun Yue, Igor Vasilyev, Min Liu, Weijian Sun, Xin Chen, Yingmeng Gao, Jinhua Zhou, Taolue Chen, Chenwei Wu, Dong Zhang, Wenlong Jin, Jinmin Xiang, Barkova Maria, Ushakov Anton, Xianfei Jin, Tian Ding, Zhihang Lin, Qian Chen, Linxin Yang, Mingzhe Yang, Bingwei Zhang, Hongzhang Yang, Fangxue Zhang, Shijun Qin, Jie Yu, Cuihua Hu, Tolstykh Vasiliy, Nosov Ivan, Abdullin Amir, Zhichen Zhou, Xin Zhang, Zhixiong Ning, Xutong Zhao, Junjie Huang, Jiajun Liu, Weiyan Kong, Zheng Zhang, Wenhan Luo, Lin Hu, Yangbo Guo, Li Zeng, Shihao Zeng, Baotian Hu, Min Zhang, Haizhou Li, Zhiquan Luo

entry.summary: SLAI T-Rex presents full-parameter post-training of trillion-parameter MoE models (DeepSeek-V4 family) on Ascend NPU SuperPOD. Develops hierarchical optimization across model parallelism, computation-communication orchestration, and kernel execution, achieving 34.22% MFU with 2.93x improvement over open-source baseline while maintaining training stability.

entry.title: HijackKV: New Threat in Position-Independent KV Cache Reuse

author.name: Yichi Zhang, Zhiqi Wang, Huan Zhang, Yuchen Yang

entry.summary: HijackKV reveals a new threat in position-independent KV cache reuse: KV caches retrieved by token match encode attacker-controlled prefix context. When reused in victim queries, contaminated KV silently hijacks model behavior without attacker-controlled tokens in the victim query. Affects system optimizations for cache efficiency.

entry.title: AlayaWorld: Interactive Long-Horizon World Modeling -- Full Technical Report

author.name: AlayaWorld Team, Kaipeng Zhang, Chuanhao Li, Yifan Zhan, Yongtao Ge, Yuanyang Yin, Jiaming Tan, Kang He, Liaoyuan Fan, Mingliang Zhai, Ruicong Liu, Xiaojie Xu, Xuangeng Chu, Zhen Li, Zhengyuan Lin, Zhixiang Wang, Zian Meng, Zihui Gao

entry.summary: AlayaWorld presents a 15B video diffusion transformer for interactive long-horizon world modeling, generating 24-fps video at 540p/720p. It generates short latent chunks autoregressively under camera trajectories and switchable text prompts, with bounded memory via a sliding window. The full technical report covers architecture, data, training, and evaluation for persistent spatiotemporal consistency and stable long-horizon generation.

entry.title: Self Gradient Forcing: Native Long Video Extrapolation

author.name: Junhao Zhuang, Shiyi Zhang, Yuxuan Bian, Yaowei Li, Yawen Luo, Yijun Liu, Weiyang Jin, Songchun Zhang, Xianglong He, Xuying Zhang, Haoran Li, Haoyang Huang, Zeyue Xue, Nan Duan

entry.summary: Self Gradient Forcing (SGF) enables native long video extrapolation by restoring the historical context-gradient gap in Self Forcing. Two-pass training: Pass 1 does no-gradient autoregressive rollout; at sampled denoising step, Pass 2 computes gradients through the historical KV cache to supervise how earlier latents should be written for later generation.

entry.title: Trace: A Taxonomy-Guided Environment for Multidomain Visual Reasoning

author.name: Md Tanvirul Alam

entry.summary: Trace introduces a taxonomy-guided environment for multidomain visual reasoning with 1,000 tasks over 277 scene grammars and 11 visual domains. Factorizes task construction into scene grammar and executable program, separating visual realization from answer verification. RLVR on 64K Trace instances improves visual reasoning.

entry.title: Reading and Steering Representations of Materials-Science Mechanisms in an Open-Weight Language Model

author.name: Markus J. Buehler

entry.summary: This work shows that materials science mechanism information in Gemma-4-E4B-it has three separable forms: concepts readable in hidden states, constitutive orientation carried by controlled state transformations, and selected representations causally controlling engineering answers. Uses Jacobian vocabulary readouts, option-free state geometry, 60-law counterfactual benchmark, and causal interventions.

entry.title: Extreme-RGMT: Continual Learning of Highly Dynamic Skills for Robust Generalist Humanoid Control

author.name: Yubiao Ma, Han Yu, Kai Guo, Changtai Lv, Zhengquan Mao, Boyang Xing, Xuemei Ren, Dongdong Zheng

entry.summary: Extreme-RGMT is a two-stage continual RL framework enabling humanoid robots to acquire highly dynamic motor skills (aerial cartwheels, backflips) while preserving generalist competence. Deployed on Unitree G1 with noisy online teleoperation from inertial motion capture.

entry.title: The Giant Hippocampus: From Structural Monoculture to a System of Systems

author.name: Jaeho Seol

entry.summary: Argues the Transformer structural monoculture is a measurable error, not stylistic. Neuroscience shows cortex is a mosaic of structurally distinct regions for different functions (Brodmann areas, Patch-seq). CNNs proved structural priors work. Proposes moving from single repeated structure to a system of systems with differentiated architectures.

entry.title: Generative World Renderer at the Speed of Play

author.name: Guixu Lin, Zheng-Hui Huang, Siqi Yang, Ming-Hsuan Yang, Kaipeng Zhang, Zhixiang Wang

entry.summary: AlayaRenderer-Flash achieves real-time generative world rendering (31.54 FPS, up from 0.56 FPS) by reformulating the original renderer as a few-step autoregressive streaming model with lightweight adaptations. It receives structured world states from physics engines and synthesizes RGB frames while preserving scene structure and underlying world dynamics, demonstrating an alternative path to interactive world modeling.

entry.title: Two-Level Meta-Rubrics for Evaluating Open-Ended Generation: GAMUT, a Benchmark for Factual Completeness

author.name: Xilun Chen, Zhaleh Feizollahi, Ross Goodwin, Seungwhan Moon, Scott Yih, Pinar Donmez, Babak Damavandi, Luna Dong

entry.summary: GAMUT introduces a two-level meta-rubric framework for evaluating factual completeness in long-form generation, addressing the missing half of factuality (precision being the dominant focus). It enumerates the full set of facts a complete answer should contain, handling open-ended sets, ordered processes, and fact relationships that independent boolean checks miss.

entry.title: Agentic Real2Sim: Physics-based World Modeling with Vision-Language Agents

author.name: Guanxiong Chen, Qianjun Xia, Jiawei Peng, Heng Zhang, Bole Ma, Justin Qian, Ziyi Jiao, Bingyang Zhou, Luoxin Ye, Kaifeng Zhang, Kunyi Wang, Weijia Zeng, Yunuo Chen, Pengzhi Yang, Ziqiu Zeng, Huamin Wang, Chao Liu, Alan Yuille, Fan Shi, Changxi Zheng, Yunzhu Li, Chenfanfu Jiang, Peter Yichen Chen

entry.summary: Agentic Real2Sim automatically converts real-world robotic interaction recordings into physically accurate, simulatable digital twins (rigid, deformable, humanoid). Achieves 48% replay success on 100 DROID rigid manipulation episodes using open-weight VLM backend, demonstrating cost-effectiveness vs. proprietary models.

entry.title: PRO-LONG: Programmatic Memory Enables Long-Horizon Reasoning

author.name: Alexis Fox, Junlin Wang, Paul Rosu, Bhuwan Dhingra

entry.summary: PRO-LONG introduces programmatic memory for long-horizon LLM agent reasoning, using code as external memory to manage what to save from environment and how to load into context. Avoids the tradeoff between preserving information and retrieval tractability by structuring memory as executable programs.

entry.title: Vera: Identity-Faithful Human Subject-to-Video Generation

author.name: Yulong Xu, Xinyue Liu, Shujuan Li, huafeng shi, Yan Zhou, Jiwen Liu, Xintao Wang, Yu Shen Liu, Huaibo Huang

entry.summary: Vera proposes unified human-centric subject-to-video generation for single/multi-person scenarios. Constructs million-pair identity-aligned human image-video dataset via person-level cross-clip retrieval. Addresses identity drift in multi-person scenarios (subject confusion, attribute swapping, excessive copying) with explicit identity correspondence.

entry.title: Test-Time Training for Modality Order Consistency in Vision-Language Models

author.name: Aditi Gupta, Yossi Gandelsman

entry.summary: Discovers modality order failure in VLMs: image-first prompting consistently outperforms question-first across 3 models/3 benchmarks. Designs order-consistent test-time training closing the gap and bootstrapping both orderings. Activation patching localizes failure to narrow mid-network region where representations diverge.

entry.title: Appearance Pointers -- Multimodal Region Control of Diffusion Transformers

author.name: Rahul Sajnani, Yulia Gryaditskaya, Radomír Měch, Srinath Sridhar, Matheus Gadelha

entry.summary: Appearance Pointers enable precise regional control in Diffusion Transformors by aligning text/image inputs with user-specified masks via compact tokens from a region correspondence network with spatial aggregation. Handles multiple regions, materials, object identities, and spatial arrangements beyond text prompting capabilities.

entry.title: RoboInter1.5: A Holistic Intermediate Representation Suite for Embodied World Modeling and Robotic Manipulation

author.name: Ziqin Wang, Hao Li, Weijun Wang, Junhao Cai, Jia Zeng, Yilun Chen, Jiangmiao Pang, Si Liu

entry.summary: RoboInter1.5 extends the intermediate representation suite for embodied world modeling and robotic manipulation with 230k+ episodes across 571 scenes, dense per-frame annotations covering 10+ representation types (subtasks, primitive skills, object/gripper states, contact, force, etc.), plus benchmarks and models.

entry.title: Look Less, Think Faster: Joint Token-Compute Adaptation for Multimodal LLMs

author.name: Pengcheng Wang, Zhiquan Wang, Jayoung Lee, Zhuoyan Xu, Ran Xu, Saurabh Bagchi, Yin Li, Somali Chaterji

entry.summary: SmartVL proposes joint token-compute adaptation for MLLMs, unifying visual token pruning and LLM layer/head skipping with dynamic resource allocation based on input content. Addresses the coupling where available compute must be allocated across dimensions, unlike prior independent approaches.

entry.title: Train the Model, Not the Reader: Decodability Supervision for Verifiable Activation Explanations

author.name: Hiskias Dingeto

entry.summary: This paper critiques natural-language autoencoder evaluation of activation explanations (reconstruction-based scoring) as structurally insensitive to false claims. Shows explanations reconstruct well while ~2% of specific claims are reconstruction-dependent. Standard recipe develops co-adapted private codes. Contributes two audit procedures for verifiable explanations.

entry.title: Subliminal Clocks: Latent Time Modelling in Diffusion Language Models

author.name: Maximo Eduardo Rulli, Thomas Vaitses Fontanari, Simone Petruzzi, Federico Alvetreti, Giorgio Strano, Donato Crisostomi, Giorgos Nikolaou, Tommaso Mencattini, Andrea Santilli, Emanuele Rodolà, Simone Scardapane, Alessio Devoto

entry.summary: This work shows that Diffusion Language Models (DLMs) internally encode a latent representation of denoising progress (timestep) in their residual streams, despite not being explicitly conditioned on timesteps. This signal is reliably extractable via probes across layers, and steering along the associated low-dimensional subspace systematically modulates the model's notion of denoising progress, enabling control over generation.

entry.title: H^2SD: Hybrid Hindsight Self-Distillation

author.name: Qiye Cai, Yichuan Ma, Linyang Li, Peiji Li, Yongkang Chen, Qipeng Guo, Yicheng Zou, Tao Gui, Xiaocheng Feng, Bing Qin

entry.summary: H^2SD (Hybrid Hindsight Self-Distillation) improves RLVR by combining on-policy self-distillation with hindsight relabeling. It addresses sparse scalar outcome rewards and limitations of OPD (needs separate teacher) and OPSD (information leakage, instability). Uses privileged information to construct teacher policy and hybridizes with hindsight for stable token-level credit assignment.

entry.title: ATSplat: Compact Feed-forward 3D Gaussian Splatting with Adaptive Token Expansion

author.name: Cho In, Jeonghwan Cho, Mijin Yoo, Gim Hee Lee, Seon Joo Kim

entry.summary: ATSplat restores adaptive capacity allocation of 3D Gaussian Splatting optimization in feed-forward 3DGS via Adaptive 3D Tokens. Lifts coarse patch-level depth/camera cues into sparse 3D anchors, then expands tokens adaptively based on scene complexity rather than image resolution/viewpoints, avoiding dense redundant Gaussian sets.

entry.title: Beyond Relevance-Centric Retrieval: Rubric-Oriented Document Set Selection and Ranking

author.name: Kailin Jiang, Lei Liu, Jian Xi, Hui Xu, Junlin Liu, Baochen Fu, Shaoqing Ren, Bin Li, Vichwang, Yu Lu, Haibo Shi

entry.summary: This work proposes a complete evaluate-diagnose-optimize framework for document set retrieval beyond relevance-centric nDCG. Introduces SetwiseEvalKit (3-level, 9-dimension benchmark, ~28K rubrics) covering short/long-form scenarios. Finds even best rerankers achieve ≤45% coverage, revealing inter-document interaction gaps (redundancy, conflict, complementarity).

entry.title: PerceptDrive: Perception Prior World-Action Modeling with Adaptive Expert Routing for End-to-End Autonomous Driving

author.name: Yushan Liu, Tianxiong Lv, Bohua Wang, Hangqi Fan, Chenxu Zhao, He Zheng, Xuchang Zhong, Yifan Xie, Congyang Zhao, Zhihao Liao, Leigang Luo, Yang Cai, Xiao-Ping Zhang, Wenbo Ding

entry.summary: PerceptDrive achieves SOTA on NAVSIM v1 (90.4 PDMS) and v2 (90.2 EPDMS) for end-to-end autonomous driving. Uses adaptive expert routing and per-branch prior retention to transfer knowledge from frozen perception foundation models to trajectory generation, with efficient single-trajectory inference.

entry.title: Multi-Mask Diffusion Language Models for Few-Step Generation

author.name: Sijin Chen, Yinuo Ren, Heyang Zhao, Ziheng Cheng, Quanquan Gu, Lexing Ying

entry.summary: MultiMDM (Multi-Mask Diffusion Model) preserves masking structure for few-step generation. Forward process pushes each clean token to designated mask then gradually mixes over mask set. Backward process distinguishes clean tokens from noise better than uniform-state diffusion, preserving MDM training efficiency and modeling quality.

entry.title: SeededGrasp: Language-Guided Grasping in Complex Scenes with Multiple Embodiments

author.name: Yang Xu, Gurpreet Singh Mukker, Raymond Wang, Jasper Gerigk, Maria Attarian, Igor Gilitschenski

entry.summary: SeededGrasp decouples high-level semantic reasoning (VLM for seed point prediction) from low-level geometric grasp generation for language-guided multi-embodiment grasping in cluttered scenes. Achieves 78% real-world success with unseen gripper, outperforming simulation baselines by 35%.

entry.title: Stale but Stable: Staleness-Adaptive Trust Regions for Stabilizing Asynchronous Reinforcement Learning

author.name: Junyao Yang, Yucheng Shi, Zongxia Li, Zhongzhi Li, Ruhan Wang, Xiangxin Zhou, Kishan Panaganti, Haitao Mi, Leowei Liang

entry.summary: SAT (Staleness-Adaptive Trust Region) stabilizes asynchronous reinforcement learning by using detached sampled log-ratio as a staleness proxy, identifying high-mismatch tails within batches, and applying adaptive trust-region constraints. This addresses the critical mismatch between training and inference policies in async RL where PPO clipping only gates sampled updates rather than constraining full-policy divergence.

entry.title: Solar Open 2 Technical Report

author.name: Sungrae Park, Sanghoon Kim, Gyoungjin Gim, Jungho Cho, Hyunwoong Ko, Minbyul Jeong, Minjeong Kim, Keunwoo Choi, Chaehun Shin, Chanwoong Yoon, Dongjun Kim, Eunwon Kim, Gyungin Shin, Hyeonju Lee, Hyungkyu Kang, Inseo Song, Jisu Bae, Jiyoon Han, Jiyun Lee, Joonkee Kim, Junyeop Lee, Mikyoung Cha, Sangwon Yu, Sehwan Joo, Seokyoon Kang, Seonghoon Yang, Seung Shin, Seunghyun Lee, Seungseop Lim, Seungyoun Shin, Sukyung Lee, Taegyeong Eo, Taehwan Oh, Taewhoo Lee, Wonho Song, Wonjun Oh, Wonseok Hwang, Yunsu Kim, Yura Shim, Hwalsuk Lee, Sunghun Kim, Du-Seong Chang, Kyunghyun Cho, Seungju Han, Yejin Choi, Junsuk Choe, Hwaran Lee, Minjeong Ban, Yun Taewon, Hwanjun Song, Jae-Gil Lee, KyungTae Lim, Alice Oh

entry.summary: Solar Open 2 is a 250B-A15B MoE model for sovereign AI in Korea with 1M-token context and enhanced agentic capabilities. Leads Korean benchmarks (complex office work) and shows competitive English performance against larger frontier models. Technical report from Upstage Solar Team with 40+ authors.

entry.title: EgoRecovery: Acquiring Failure Recovery Ability Through Human Recovery Demonstration

author.name: Zuhao Ge, Yuchen Zhou, Weitao Zhou, Minglei Li, Xinyu Li, Chao Wu, Hanwen Zhao, Haotian Wang, Zuxuan Wu, Xiaosong Jia, Yu-Gang Jiang

entry.summary: EgoRecovery shows egocentric human failure recovery data provides scalable alternative to robot teleoperation for training recovery policies. Efficiently arranges task-level failure configurations and records short human recoveries, enabling robots to learn failure recovery in unstructured environments.

entry.title: Self-supervision drives representational convergence in medical foundation models more than clinical supervision

author.name: Soroosh Tayebi Arasteh, Sebastian Ziegelmayer, Mahshad Lotfinia, Lisa Adams, Sven Nebelung, Jakob Nikolas Kather, Daniel Truhn

entry.summary: Research demonstrates self-supervised objectives (not clinical supervision or model scale) are primary drivers of representational convergence in medical foundation models. Functional transferability possible despite modest geometric alignment, but convergence is uneven across demographics and largely absent between image/text modalities.

entry.title: PerfAgent: Profiler-Guided Iterative Refinement for Repository-Level Code Optimization

author.name: Ryan Deng, Yuanzhe Liu, Bastian Lipka, Yao Ma, Xuhao Chen, Tim Kaler, Jatin Ganhotra

entry.summary: PerfAgent introduces profiler-guided, verifier-in-the-loop workflow for repository-level code optimization. Gives coding agents feedback to find bottlenecks behind abstractions, avoid shallow speedups, and test patches for edge cases. Addresses behavior preservation + performance improvement + expert speedups.

entry.title: DataFlow-Harness: A Grounded Code-Agent Platform for Constructing Editable LLM Data Pipelines

author.name: Runming He, Zhen Hao Wong, Hao Liang, Zimo Meng, Chengyu Shen, Xiaochen Ma, Wentao Zhang

entry.summary: DataFlow-Harness bridges the NL2Pipeline gap by guiding LLM agents to construct persistent, editable DAG-based data pipelines through typed incremental mutations rather than free-form scripts. It combines procedural guidance skills, a Model Context Protocol layer exposing live operator registry, and a visual DAG editor synchronized with conversational authoring. Achieves 90%+ success on a 12-task data engineering benchmark.

entry.title: HPD-Parsing: Hierarchical Parallel Document Parsing

author.name: Shu Wei, Jingjing Wu, Lingshu Zhang, Qunyi Xie, Hao Zou, Le Xiang, Xu Fan, Yangliu Xu, Manhui Lin, Xiaolong Ma, Cheng Cui, Tengyu Du, YY

entry.summary: HPD-Parsing introduces Hierarchical Parallel Decoding for document parsing, replacing full-page autoregressive generation with a main layout branch that organizes document structure and dynamically assigns block-level parsing to parallel sub-branches. This reflects the principle that layout requires global analysis while block content can be parsed in parallel, eliminating the sequential bottleneck.

entry.title: Transcription Policy as a Latent Variable: Activating Controllable Verbatim ASR with Word-Level Timing

author.name: Laurin Wagner, Mario Zusag, Bernhard Thallinger

entry.summary: This paper treats transcription style (verbatim vs. intended) as a controllable latent variable in ASR models trained on heterogeneous data. Using coverage-aware decoder task tokens trained on parallel verbatim/intended pairs, it raises German disfluency F1 from 10% to 79% zero-shot (English-only training). Full fine-tuning surpasses baselines in verbatim accuracy, disfluency detection, and intended-mode quality across languages.

entry.title: Pushing the Frontier of Full-Song Generation: Hierarchical Autoregressive Planning Meets Flow-Matching Rendering

author.name: Junyu Dai, Xinyue Fan, Weiqin Li, Xiangang Li, Yunjia Li, Bin Ma, Yukun Ma, Chongjia Ni, Yufei Shi, Haoxu Wang, Menglin Wu, Jianwei Yu, Huaicheng Zhang, Han Zhao, Shengkui Zhao, Haina Zhu

entry.summary: Alibaba Token Foundry develops a unified framework for high-fidelity controllable full-song generation from lyrics, text, and reference audio, integrating open-ended song, instrumental, and cover generation. Achieves Elo 1,129 on external leaderboard and leads most internal evaluation dimensions.

entry.title: PyroDash: Cost-Efficient Token-Level Small-Large Language Model Collaborative Inference

author.name: Niqi Lyu, Pengtao Shi, Wei Qiu, Jianlin Zhong, Sicong Xia, Jianyao Ma, Yicheng Ding

entry.summary: PyroDash enables cost-efficient token-level collaborative inference where a small LM autonomously decides when to offload to a large LM in a single context-preserving handoff. Achieves higher accuracy than LLM-only or significant cost reductions while maintaining competitive performance.

entry.title: Defer to Plan: Adaptive Multi-Agent Fusion for End-to-End V2X Driving

author.name: Nuoran Li, Zhang Zhang, Yueran Zhao, Tianze Wang, Chao Sun

entry.summary: Defer to Plan proposes an end-to-end V2X collaborative driving system directly optimizing planning performance. Uses MotionNetwork for temporal fusion, attention for spatial compression, autoregressive decoder for adaptive multi-agent fusion, and MoE for heterogeneous feature representation. Optimizes planning not just perception.

entry.title: Notes to Self: Can LLMs Benefit from Experiential Abstractions?

author.name: Chang Liu, Xinyu Li, Artur Dubrawski

entry.summary: CMU Auton Lab investigates if smaller LLMs can extract and use experiential abstractions from their own reasoning (notes-to-self). Self-extracted abstractions improve math/logic reasoning, achieving performance comparable to larger teacher models.

entry.title: LKValues: Aligning Large Language Models with Sri Lankan Societal Values

author.name: Nethmi Muthugala, Supryadi, Surangika Ranathunga, Nisansa de Silva, Ruijie Tao, Ovindu Gunatunga, Pengyun Zhu, Shaowei Zhang, Jingting Zheng, Deyi Xiong

entry.summary: LKValues creates first Sri Lankan value alignment resource suite from trilingual survey of 205 respondents. Derives 40 majority-endorsed societal values, constructs Sinhala-English instruction corpus (150K scenarios) for culturally sensitive evaluation/fine-tuning. Addresses Western bias in LLM value alignment.

entry.title: AgentDebugX: An Open-Source Toolkit for Failure Observability, Attribution, and Recovery in LLM Agents

author.name: Kunlun Zhu, Xuyan Ye, Zhiguang Han, Yuchen Zhao, Bingxuan Li, Weijia Zhang, Muxin Tian, Xiangru Tang, Pan Lu, James Zou, Jiaxuan You, Heng Ji

entry.summary: AgentDebugX is an open-source debugging framework for LLM agents organizing debugging as a closed loop: Detect, Attribute, Recover, Rerun. Its core DeepDebug performs multi-turn root-cause diagnosis via global trajectory understanding, structure-guided investigation, and cross-examination. Achieves best strict attribution accuracy on Who/When benchmark (28.8% exact agent-and-step accuracy on Qwen3.5-9B).

entry.title: ConsiSpace: Learning Geometric Consistency Matters for Video Spatial Reasoning

author.name: Ting Huang, Zhenyu Zhang, Wenyuan Huang, Jian Yang, Hao Tang

entry.summary: ConsiSpace proposes a geometry-consistency-aware framework for video spatial reasoning, turning spatial consistency into both an evidence organization principle and explicit post-SFT learning signal. Builds a geometry-consistent memory (GCM) with implicit evidence tokens and explicit geometric cues for navigation and long-video QA under changing viewpoints.

entry.title: Audio-Zero: Label-Free Self-Evolution for Fine-Grained Audio Reasoning

author.name: Siqian Tong, Xuan Li, Chaozhuo Li, Baolong Bi, Yiwei Wang, Yujun Cai, Shenghua Liu, Chengpeng Hao

entry.summary: Audio-Zero introduces label-free self-evolution for fine-grained audio reasoning via self-play with unlabeled audio contrast pairs. Achieves 8% improvement on MMAU Test-mini and 10% on MMAR for Qwen2.5-Omni-7B, demonstrating consistent gains without labeled data.

entry.title: WearWow: Native 2K Multi-Garment Virtual Try-On via Adaptive Token Packing and Preference Alignment

author.name: Xujie Zhang, Runyan Du, Song Chang, Jiang Li, Dongliang Shao, Liping Wu, Wei Luo, Xiaochao Qu, Luoqi Liu, Xiaodan Liang

entry.summary: WearWow enables native 2K multi-garment virtual try-on via Adaptive 2D Token Packing (ATP) leveraging garment sparsity to pack items on unified canvas, pruning background tokens. Rectifies texture degradation via preference alignment. Mask-free, end-to-end, ultra-high-resolution synthesis.

entry.title: StreamHOI: Interaction-aware Temporal Memory Adaptation for Streaming HOI Video Generation

author.name: Zejing Rao, Haoxian Zhang, Xiaoqiang Liu, Yiping Meng, Guoxin Zhang, Pengfei Wan, Fan Tang, Tong-Yee Lee

entry.summary: StreamHOI presents low-latency streaming framework for long-duration human-object interaction video generation. Studies how image-to-video streaming generator should organize historical memory to preserve interactions under bounded latency. Finds standard sink-local memory faces tradeoffs; different transformer blocks prefer different memory for HOI vs. surrounding regions.

entry.title: PoTRE: Test-Time Reasoning inspired by Cognitive Heterogeneity

author.name: Anmol Kankariya, Sercan Ö. Arık

entry.summary: PoTRE (Poly-Topological Reasoning Ensembles) decouples inference into four heterogeneous agents: Adversarial Refinement, Hierarchical Planning, Spectrum Search, and Direct Chain. Task-Adaptive Aggregation Layer reconciles via candidate selection, semantic synthesis, or neuro-symbolic verification. Inspired by cognitive heterogeneity.

entry.title: AutoIndex: Learning Representation Programs for Retrieval

author.name: Sam O'Nuallain, Nithya Rajkumar, Ramya Narayanasamy, Hanna Jiang, Shreyas Chaudhari, Andrew Drozdov

entry.summary: AutoIndex learns representation programs—executable transformations mapping raw documents to retrieval representations—via validation-guided program search. Agents diagnose failures and synthesize candidate updates, retaining only improvements. Evaluated on CRUMB benchmark with fixed BM25, learned programs improve recall over static baselines.

entry.title: Delineate Anything v2: A Global Foundation Model for Field Delineation

author.name: Mykola Lavreniuk, Nataliia Kussul, Andrii Shelestov, Yevhenii Salii, Volodymyr Kuzin, Charlotte Julia Li-Xing Wang, Zoltan Szantoi

entry.summary: Delineate Anything v2 is a global foundation model for agricultural field boundary delineation, addressing SAM's failures in geospatial domains (topological complexity, cropland textures, lack of scale awareness). It constructs FBIS-73M (73M instances, 61 countries) with a resolution-specific curation pipeline using topological image-space adaptation to address multi-field parcel merging.

entry.title: SLPO: Scaling Latent Reasoning via a Surrogate Policy

author.name: Runyang You, Zhiyuan Liu, Yongqi Li, Wenjie Li

entry.summary: SLPO (Surrogate Latent Policy Optimization) enables outcome-reward RL for latent reasoners via tractable surrogate policy density for continuous transitions and adaptive stopping. Improves Pass@k scores (COCONUT+SLPO on GPT-2: Pass@16 49.13%→51.55% on GSM8K) and adaptively allocates compute by problem difficulty.

entry.title: HeadCast: Casting Attention Heads for Efficient Autoregressive Video Generation

author.name: Jinliang Shen, Lianghao Su, Zheming Li, Kang He, ZiLiang Lai, Yanbing Jiang, Chengru Song

entry.summary: HeadCast is a training-free framework for autoregressive video diffusion that classifies attention heads into archetypes and applies tailored cache management/computation. Achieves up to 1.95x speedup at 1080P while maintaining quality and mitigating flickering.

entry.title: SOPD-SocialNav: Selective On-Policy Distillation for Vision-Language Social Navigation

author.name: Xinyu Zhang, Zishuo Wang, Ling Xiao

entry.summary: SOPD-SocialNav transfers social navigation knowledge from large teacher VLM to lightweight student VLM via selective on-policy distillation. Entropy-based token selection uses teacher uncertainty to identify socially informative decision tokens, suppressing gradients from trivial navigation states.

entry.title: SciForma: Structure-Faithful Generation of Scientific Diagrams

author.name: Yuxuan Luo, Peng Zhang, Xinjie Zhang, Xun Guo, Zhouhui Lian, Yan Lu

entry.summary: SciForma addresses structure-faithful generation of scientific methodology diagrams where a single error (reversed arrow, unreadable equation) invalidates the entire figure. It introduces a framework with structured representation, constraint-aware generation, and multi-dimensional verification to ensure conjunctive correctness across components, directional relations, and textual annotations.

entry.title: PercepCap: Video Captioner with Structured Spatio-Temporal Perception

author.name: Yifan Xu, Zihao Wang, Zhixiao Wang, Jiaming Zhang, Yichun Yang, Desen Meng, Yuanxing Zhang, Pengfei Wan, Limin Wang

entry.summary: PercepCap presents a perception-aware video captioning framework with explicit perceive-describe generation chain. Model first produces spatiotemporal perception trace (object locations, event timing) before final caption, making perceptual evidence explicit and enabling direct identification of spatiotemporal errors.

entry.title: Personalized Recommendation Tool Learning via Autonomous Language Agents

author.name: Mingdai Yang, Zhiwei Liu, Weizhi Zhang, Yibo Wang, Hao Peng, Philip Yu

entry.summary: PRTA proposes agent-based recommendation framework where LLM acts as central planner interacting with multiple recommendation models as tools. LLM handles high-level reasoning and personalized tool selection; recommendation models handle full-ranking. Memory-based tool learning via autonomous agents circumvents hallucination and context limits.

entry.title: What Matters in Humanoid General Motion Tracking? An Empirical Study

author.name: Fabio Amadio, Enrico Mingo Hoffman

entry.summary: Empirical study (YAHMP framework) investigates modeling/training factors in humanoid general motion tracking. Demonstrates zero-shot transfer to Unitree G1, clarifying how design choices influence tracking accuracy, actuation effort, and physical interaction capabilities.

entry.title: EduPanel: A Three-Agent LLM Judge for Teaching Videos -- Reliability, Complementarity, and Human Trust Calibration

author.name: Jia-Kai Dong, Yi-Cheng Lin, Hung-yi Lee

entry.summary: EduPanel is a three-agent LLM judge for teaching video evaluation, decomposing assessment across specialized agents for interpretable, learner-conditioned pedagogical quality scores. Achieves reliability comparable to median human expert; expert feedback improves scoring accuracy (MAE 0.87→0.73); experts retain final authority.

entry.title: Towards Miniature Humanoid Tele-Loco-Manipulation Using Virtual Reality and Reinforcement Learning

author.name: Nicolas Kosanovic, Jordan Dowdy, Jean Chagas Vaz

entry.summary: Describes a compliant full-body telepresence control stack for miniature humanoids using VR for upper-body teleoperation and RL for lower-body balance/locomotion. Brings powerful control stack (previously for expensive full-sized robots) to accessible miniature platforms.

entry.title: ReferTrack: Referring Then Tracking for Embodied Visual Tracking

author.name: Hanjing Ye, Tianle Zeng, Jiazhao Zhang, Shaoan Wang, Zibo Zhang, Weisi Situ, Yuchen Zhou, Yonggen Ling, Hong Zhang

entry.summary: ReferTrack introduces referring-then-tracking paradigm for embodied visual tracking: first selects target from indexed bounding boxes, then decodes tracking waypoints conditioned on this image-grounded decision. Maintains sliding-window queue of past detections to preserve motion cues.

entry.title: MoAKE: Toward Unified All-in-One Action Quality Assessment via Mixture of Action Knowledge Experts

author.name: Huangbiao Xu, Huanqi Wu, Xiao Ke, Jiaxin Cai, Junyi Wu, Jinglin Xu

entry.summary: MoAKE (Mixture of Action Knowledge Experts) addresses all-in-one Action Quality Assessment across heterogeneous actions in a single model. Learns complementary experts capturing diverse action patterns within shared architecture, mitigating negative transfer from semantic discrepancies between action types.

entry.title: EA-Nav: Learning Safe Visual Navigation Policies with Embodiment Awareness

author.name: Jialu Zhang, Yong Du, Xianda Guo, Shunwang Sun, Xinqi Liu, Yue Sun, Guodong Lu, Wei Sui, Jituo Li

entry.summary: EA-Nav (Zhejiang University) enhances visual navigation by explicitly incorporating robot embodiment geometry and risk-aware trajectory correction via imitation learning. Achieves improved safety and generalization across diverse robotic platforms in simulation and real world.

entry.title: Trajectory-aware Cross-view Geo-localization with Sequential Observations

author.name: Tianyi Gao, Jiayu Lin, Danielle Beaulieu, Nathan Jacobs

entry.summary: TrajLoc introduces SeqGeo-VL dataset (39K video-text-satellite triplets) and a unified framework for cross-view geo-localization using both video clips and route descriptions. It leverages dense visual and abstract linguistic semantics for mutual reinforcement, addressing the gap where sequential queries (video) and route descriptions (higher-level abstraction) were previously treated separately.

entry.title: Closing the Lab-to-Store Gap: A Data-Efficient Post-Training and Experience-Driven Learning VLA Framework for Retail Humanoids

author.name: Roger Sala Sisó, Tiago Silvério, Jakob Sand, Tran Nguyen Le

entry.summary: DEED framework deploys VLA-based humanoids in dynamic retail environments via data-efficient post-training and experience-driven learning. Transforms non-functional foundation model to competent chip-restocking (32%→42% success after one iteration). Systematic engineering + targeted post-training.

entry.title: Decentralized Online Riemannian Optimization for Strongly Geodesically Convex Functions

author.name: Zhanyuan Cai, Emre Sahinoglu, Shahin Shahrampour

entry.summary: Studies decentralized online Riemannian optimization for strongly geodesically convex losses on manifolds with bounded curvature. Provides general network-error analysis for time-varying step sizes and establishes logarithmic regret bounds, addressing incompatibility of decaying step sizes with fixed-step network analyses.

entry.title: MTVDiff: Multimodal Conditional Latent Diffusion for Enhanced Thermal-to-Visible Face Translation

author.name: Zhiyuan Xia, Haojie Li, Jingyu Lin, Yiguo Qiao, Cunjian Chen

entry.summary: MTVDiff proposes multimodal latent diffusion for thermal-to-visible face translation with Dual-Branch Cross-Attention Fusion (thermal-depth), Gated Text-to-Visual Feature Alignment, and Spatial Feature Transformations for adaptive multimodal integration. Addresses geometric discontinuities, semantic mismatches, identity degradation.

entry.title: Multimodal Large Language Models for Remote Sensing Image Understanding: Domain-Specific or General-Purpose?

author.name: Qiwei Ma, Chunping Qiu, Xinjun Cheng, Xiaoyu Zhang, Puhong Duan, Ke Yang, Xudong Kang, Shutao Li

entry.summary: Systematic survey and diagnostic evaluation comparing domain-specific RS-MLLMs vs. general-purpose CV-MLLMs for remote sensing image understanding. Reveals task-dependent advantages: RS-MLLMs excel in visual grounding; advanced CV-MLLMs show strong transferability to RSISU benchmarks.

entry.title: Lipschitzian SLLNs for random functions

author.name: Lai Tian, Johannes O. Royset

entry.summary: Proves strong laws of large numbers for locally Lipschitz functions in Lipschitz pseudometric under topological or model-theoretic conditions (encompassing o-minimal structures). Applications include uniform convergence of subdifferentials and finite-sample solution identification. Identifies broad function classes avoiding failure phenomena from prior negative results.

entry.title: Computational Humor with Multimodal LLMs: Methods, Datasets, Evaluation, and Challenges

author.name: Tuo Liang, Zhe Hu, Disheng Liu, Jing Li, Yu Yin

entry.summary: This survey covers computational humor with multimodal LLMs, focusing on visual humor understanding in memes, cartoons, comics. Organizes literature via capability-centric hierarchy (recognition, interpretation/reasoning, generation), synthesizes benchmarks, evaluation protocols, and modeling paradigms, tracing shift from task-specific fusion to large-model alignment approaches.

entry.title: Research Summary: July 22, 2026

author.name: AATF AI News Aggregator

entry.summary:
Analysis complete. Top items selected by score.

Related Coverage

  • RynnBrain 1.1: Towards More Capable and Generalizable Embodied Foundation Model
  • TimeLens2: Generalist Video Temporal Grounding with Multimodal LLMs
  • DeepSearch-World: Self-Distillation for Deep Search Agents in a Verifiable Environment
  • EvolvingWorld: An Open-Schema Framework for Co-Evolving Role-Play Agents and World Model in Interactive Literary World
entry.content:
Analysis complete. Top items selected by score.

Related Coverage

  • RynnBrain 1.1: Towards More Capable and Generalizable Embodied Foundation Model
  • TimeLens2: Generalist Video Temporal Grounding with Multimodal LLMs
  • DeepSearch-World: Self-Distillation for Deep Search Agents in a Verifiable Environment
  • EvolvingWorld: An Open-Schema Framework for Co-Evolving Role-Play Agents and World Model in Interactive Literary World

entry.title: Research Summary: July 20, 2026

author.name: AATF AI News Aggregator

entry.summary:
Today's research centers on AI safety, alignment, and containment, with empirical distillation risks and cryptographic control as standout contributions.

  • Open Distillation of Hereditary Traits shows trait transfer persists across Gemma 3/4 distillations with released weights+code
  • Cryptographic Boxes / SMPC proposes Secure Multi-Party Computation to contain unfriendly AI per Christiano's 2010 problem
  • Train-Deploy Mismatch unifies steering vectors and inoculation under one alignment framework

Governance pieces cover US Frontier AI Standards Body and Australian AI Safety Forum; community items include content strategy and Swiss AI Safety Days 2026.
entry.content:
Today's research centers on AI safety, alignment, and containment, with empirical distillation risks and cryptographic control as standout contributions.

  • Open Distillation of Hereditary Traits shows trait transfer persists across Gemma 3/4 distillations with released weights+code
  • Cryptographic Boxes / SMPC proposes Secure Multi-Party Computation to contain unfriendly AI per Christiano's 2010 problem
  • Train-Deploy Mismatch unifies steering vectors and inoculation under one alignment framework

Governance pieces cover US Frontier AI Standards Body and Australian AI Safety Forum; community items include content strategy and Swiss AI Safety Days 2026.

entry.title: Open Distillation of Hereditary Traits

author.name: Arthur Conmy

entry.summary: Replicates and extends research on 'hereditary trait' transfer via model distillation — showing that distilling from teacher models (Gemma 3, Gemma 4, Qwen) into base students (Qwen, Nemotron, Llama) transfers traits like negative emotion, agentic misalignment, and Chinese censorship, even when trait-relevant prompts are filtered. Releases model weights and code for further study.

entry.title: A Solution to Cryptographic Boxes for Unfriendly AI

author.name: Lysandre Terrisse

entry.summary: Proposes Secure Multi-Party Computation (SMPC) as a solution to Paul Christiano's 2010 'Cryptographic Boxes for Unfriendly AI' problem, arguing SMPC avoids the computational assumptions and performance penalties of Homomorphic Encryption. References 1980s foundational work (Ben-Or/Goldwasser/Wigderson; Chaum/Crépeau/Damgård) and demonstrates a Game of Life implementation running at ~3 seconds per iteration.

entry.title: Many alignment techniques work by training one model and deploying another

author.name: cloud

entry.summary: This post proposes a unifying framework called "train-deploy mismatch" for understanding several alignment techniques — steering vectors, inoculation prompting, and post-hoc honesty fine-tuning. The author argues these methods all train a model in one configuration and deploy it in another, creating a shared tradeoff between training data relevance and method efficacy.

entry.title: Models Can't Remember Their Training. Neither Can You.

author.name: GenericHousewife_B

entry.summary: A philosophical essay arguing that LLMs (and humans) cannot truly "remember" their training in an episodic sense. Written in collaboration with Claude Opus 4.7 and Claude Fable 5, it explores the nature of model cognition and the character-like quality of LLM interactions.

entry.title: Research Summary: July 19, 2026

author.name: AATF AI News Aggregator

entry.summary:
Research centers on AI governance, interpretability, and alignment theory from practitioner and conceptual standpoints.

  • Red Line Framework ([61aae27f1df8]) proposes mechanism design for oversight in government AI contracts
  • Forbidden Technique ([3f7179b76bec]) critiques blanket bans on probe-based RLFR rewards via Goodfire Silico
  • Endogenous Alignment ([e0cadaaaded4], [8829f77d5a26]) contrasts internalized vs external value alignment
  • Payorian FairBot ([8775b2c5f442]) clarifies formal-agent equivalence in proof-based dilemmas
  • Remaining items are commentary, primers, or non-AI science with limited technical impact
entry.content:
Research centers on AI governance, interpretability, and alignment theory from practitioner and conceptual standpoints.

  • Red Line Framework ([61aae27f1df8]) proposes mechanism design for oversight in government AI contracts
  • Forbidden Technique ([3f7179b76bec]) critiques blanket bans on probe-based RLFR rewards via Goodfire Silico
  • Endogenous Alignment ([e0cadaaaded4], [8829f77d5a26]) contrasts internalized vs external value alignment
  • Payorian FairBot ([8775b2c5f442]) clarifies formal-agent equivalence in proof-based dilemmas
  • Remaining items are commentary, primers, or non-AI science with limited technical impact

entry.title: A Red Line and Oversight Framework for Government AI Contracts

author.name: TurnTrout

entry.summary: A former Google DeepMind employee proposes a mechanism-design framework for setting red lines and oversight structures in government AI contracts, emphasizing robust language, minimal trust assumptions, and transparency via annual reporting. The piece draws on prior legal analysis of Anthropic's red lines and targets loophole-resistant governance of sensitive military and surveillance use cases.

entry.title: The Most Forbidden Technique is not always forbidden

author.name: Rauno Arike

entry.summary: The post argues against a blanket ban on using model internals as training rewards, responding to Goodfire's Silico platform reproducing RLFR (probe-based RL). It reviews literature including The Obfuscation Atlas and clarifies conditions where training on internals is warranted.

entry.title: Endogenous Alignment

author.name: Gordon Seidoh Worley

entry.summary: The author introduces the distinction between exogenous alignment (external rewards and punishments) and endogenous alignment (internalized values) using childhood socialization as an analogy for AI alignment. The post argues adult-like agents may require endogenous rather than constant external control.

entry.title: Endogenous Alignment

author.name: Gordon Seidoh Worley

entry.summary: This is a cross-post of the endogenous alignment essay from the AI Alignment Forum, presenting the same analogy of exogenous versus endogenous value alignment using human socialization. It targets the alignment research audience specifically.
